mnemonics: U.E.T.C.H. (Unhandled Exceptions Terminate Code Harshly): Remember that uncaught exceptions bubble up and cause the program to crash if not handled.
keyPoints: An uncaught exception is one that is not handled by a try-catch block.
Uncaught exceptions propagate up the call stack until they are caught or reach the main method, causing program termination.
The JVM's default uncaught exception handler prints the exception stack trace to the console before terminating the program.
commonMistakes: Assuming an exception will be handled by a method further up the call stack without explicitly ensuring it is caught.
Confusing checked exceptions (which must be caught or declared) with unchecked exceptions (which can be uncaught).
Not providing any exception handling for critical operations, relying solely on the default JVM termination behavior.
